title: Randomness and seeds
text: Setting seed encourages model produce consistent output. ’s much less variation images compared unseeded set, event though results perfectly identical.       different seed value produces different consistent set images. Comparing set seed shows seed affects specific output, just consistency.       Unfortunately ’s way determine seed gemini uses don’t specify one, can’t reproduce unseeded results. want able reproduce specific image, set seed start.

what: Generate Presentation Images Using Google Gemini
title: Generate Presentation Images Using Google Gemini
text: bananarama generates slide backgrounds Google Gemini. Define images YAML configuration file support reference images style defaults, generate images parallel, making fast generate full deck images. bananarama avoids usual tedious loop copying prompts web UI, downloading images, tweaking, repeating. Now process (mostly) reproducible: prompts live version-controlled YAML, regenerating every image single function call. copy-paste losing track prompt produced image.

title: Generate Presentation Images Using Google Gemini
text: can install development version bananarama GitHub:

title: Generate Presentation Images Using Google Gemini
text: Create bananarama.yaml file describes images want generate: Reference images like [hadley] matched image files (e.g. hadley.png) directory YAML file. generate images : Images already exist skipped unless pass force = TRUE. Bananrama currently uses nano banana2 (akaa gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview), costs around $0.07 per image.
code: defaults:   style: >     Flat vector editorial illustration with a muted, desaturated     color palette. Mid-century modern aesthetic. Calm and approachable.  images:   - name: robot-factory     description: >       Draw a picture of [hadley] overseeing a factory full of robots.       The robots should be typing at computers. bananarama::bananarama("path/to/bananarama.yaml")

title: Generate Presentation Images Using Google Gemini
text: style: Style prompt appended every image description. Use ensure consistent style across slides. description: Default description; usually needed unless want experiment styles. aspect-ratio: One "1:1", "3:2", "16:9", etc. Default: "16:9". resolution: One "1K", "2K", "4K". Default: "1K". n: Number variants generate per image. Default: 1. model: Gemini model use. Default: "gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview" (aka nano banana2). force: true, regenerate images even already exist. Default: false. seed: Integer seed random number generator. Makes output (perfectly) deterministic.

title: Generate Presentation Images Using Google Gemini
text: Output directory generated images, relative YAML file. Defaults directory name YAML file (e.g. bananarama.yaml outputs bananarama/).

title: Generate Presentation Images Using Google Gemini
text: image : name (required): Used output filename ({name}.png). description (required, unless default set): Prompt image generation. Use [name] reference images directory. n: Number variants generate. Output files named {name}-1.png, {name}-2.png, etc. Default: 1. style, aspect-ratio, resolution, model, force, seed: Per-image overrides defaults.

what: bananarama: Generate Presentation Images Using Google Gemini — bananarama-package
title: bananarama: Generate Presentation Images Using Google Gemini — bananarama-package
text: Reproducibly generate slide images using Google Gemini. Define images YAML configuration file support reference images style defaults, eliminating need copy paste prompts back forth web UI. Images generated parallel speed.

title: Generate presentation images from a YAML configuration — bananarama
Generate presentation images from a YAML configuration — bananarama
text: path Path YAML configuration file directory containing bananarama.yaml. Defaults "bananarama.yaml" current directory. output_dir Directory save generated images, relative YAML configuration file (absolute path). Defaults output-dir field YAML file, directory name YAML file (e.g. bananarama.yaml outputs bananarama/). force TRUE, regenerate images even already exist.
